metadata.title: Coach — Training Intelligence
metadata.description: Smart training plans, fatigue detection, and race readiness for endurance athletes.

home.heroDescription: Coach imports your training data from GPX, FIT, or CSV files, builds personalized weekly plans for your goal races, detects fatigue before you get injured, and keeps you on track every single week.

dataImport.title: Data Import
dataImport.description: Upload GPX, TCX, FIT, or CSV files from your devices — Garmin, Wahoo, COROS, and more.

raceGoals.title: Race Goals
raceGoals.description: Set your target races — 100km ultra, marathon, triathlon — with distance, elevation, and date.

weeklyPlans.title: Weekly Plans
weeklyPlans.description: Every Sunday, get a personalized training plan for the week ahead, built around your schedule.

fatigueDetection.title: Fatigue Detection
fatigueDetection.description: We monitor 8 fatigue signals — TSB, HR drift, monotony, strain — and warn you before you break down.

readinessScore.title: Readiness Score
readinessScore.description: A single 0-100 score combining fitness, fatigue, volume adherence, and consistency.

trajectoryTracking.title: Trajectory Tracking
trajectoryTracking.description: See if your training ramp is on track to hit your goal — and get adjustments if it isn't.

home.ctaTitle: Ready to take your training seriously?
home.ctaDescription: No more guessing. Let data drive your training decisions.

dashboard.enableDetailedMetricsDesc: Trackpoint-level analysis provides deeper insights but requires more processing power.
dashboard.readinessDialogP1: Readiness is a composite score (0–100) that blends your chronic training load (CTL), acute fatigue (ATL), volume adherence, and recovery signals into a single at-a-glance metric.
dashboard.readinessDialogP2: 70–100: Ready to train hard — your body is recovered and adapted.
dashboard.readinessDialogP3: 50–69: Proceed with caution — consider an easier session or extra recovery.
dashboard.readinessDialogP4: 0–49: High fatigue or low recovery — prioritize rest or very light activity.
dashboard.readinessDialogP5: The volume adherence bar shows how well your recent volume matches your training plan target.
dashboard.ctlDialogP1: CTL is a rolling 42-day weighted average of your daily TSS (Training Stress Score). It represents your fitness level — the cumulative effect of all training over the last ~6 weeks.
dashboard.ctlDialogP2: Think of it as your "training baseline." Higher values mean you're maintaining a larger training load. Consistent CTL growth indicates improving aerobic fitness.
dashboard.ctlDialogP3: Formula: TSS × e^(-t/42) weighted sum, where t is days ago.
dashboard.atlDialogP1: ATL is a rolling 7-day weighted average of your daily TSS. It represents your fatigue level — the acute load from your most recent training.
dashboard.atlDialogP2: High ATL (>80) means you're in a heavy training block and may be accumulating significant fatigue. This is normal during build phases but needs to be balanced with recovery.
dashboard.atlDialogP3: Formula: TSS × e^(-t/7) weighted sum, where t is days ago.
dashboard.tsbDialogP1: TSB is the difference between CTL (fitness) and ATL (fatigue): TSB = CTL − ATL.
dashboard.tsbDialogP2: A positive TSB means training load has been light recently — you're fresh and recovered, ideal for racing or key sessions.
dashboard.tsbDialogP3: A negative TSB means fatigue exceeds fitness — you're in a training overload phase. Moderate negatives (−5 to −15) are normal during building blocks. Deeper negatives (<−20) suggest you may need a deload week soon.
dashboard.tsbDialogP4: Target race-day TSB: +5 to +15 (tapered and ready).
dashboard.hrDecouplingDialogP1: Heart rate decoupling measures how efficiently your heart rate responds during steady-state exercise. As you fatigue, your heart rate drifts upward (cardiac drift) to maintain the same output — this drift is "decoupling."
dashboard.hrDecouplingDialogP2: Excellent (<5%): Highly efficient aerobic system — your heart rate barely drifts.
dashboard.hrDecouplingDialogP3: Good (5–10%): Normal range for most athletes. Some drift is expected.
dashboard.hrDecouplingDialogP4: Elevated (>10%): Significant drift — possible heat stress, dehydration, insufficient aerobic fitness, or overtraining.
dashboard.hrDecouplingDialogP5: Calculated from HR vs. pace/power regression over the last 20 minutes of steady-state efforts.
dashboard.efDialogP1: Efficiency Factor measures how much speed (or power) you produce per heart beat. It's calculated as pace (m/s) divided by heart rate for runs, or power (watts) divided by heart rate for rides.
dashboard.efDialogP2: A rising EF over time means you're getting fitter — you can go faster at the same heart rate, or maintain the same pace at a lower heart rate. This is the hallmark of aerobic adaptation.
dashboard.efDialogP3: Use it to track long-term fitness trends rather than day-to-day changes, since hydration, heat, fatigue, and caffeine all cause normal short-term variation.
dashboard.efDialogP4: Tracked as a rolling weekly average using your steady-state efforts.

detail.title: Activity Details
detail.paceGapLegend: Pace (solid blue) · GAP (solid green) · Raw power (thin amber) · Smoothed power (bold amber)

goals.title: Goals
goals.noGoalsDesc: Set your first race goal to get personalized training plans.
goals.goalStatementPlaceholder: What do you want to accomplish? e.g. "Finish under 12 hours"

integration.title: Connect Your Watch
integration.description: Sync your training data automatically by connecting your wearable account. Choose one or skip — you can set this up later.
integration.explanation: Syncing your watch auto-imports activities so you don't have to upload files manually. Garmin also syncs daily health metrics like heart rate, sleep, and HRV.

review.title: Schedule Your Training Review
review.description: Set when the AI Coach analyzes your training and generates insights and next week's plan.
review.cardDescription: How often and when the AI Coach generates fresh analysis and next week's plan
review.frequencyLabel: How often should the AI analyze your training?
review.explanation: The AI Coach reviews your training logs, fatigue, readiness, and goal progress on this schedule. It generates personalized coach notes and adjusts your weekly plan. Weekly is recommended for most athletes.

goal.title: Set Your First Goal
goal.description: Tell the coach what you're training for. You can always add more goals or adjust later.
goal.savedDescription: You can add more goals from the Settings page later.
goal.explanation: Setting goals powers the AI coach's race readiness analysis — it tracks your training trajectory against the target and tells you if you're on pace, need more work, or are falling behind. You can always add more goals later.

bodyMetrics.title: Track Your Body Metrics
bodyMetrics.description: Enter your current weight and optional body stats to improve analysis accuracy.
bodyMetrics.explanation: Body weight is used to calculate relative intensity metrics (e.g., watts/kg, VO₂ estimates). Tracking your resting heart rate helps detect fatigue trends. Even a one-time entry improves your analysis.

ingestion.title: Import Training Data
ingestion.description: Import training data from GPX, TCX, FIT, CSV files, or enter manually

fileUpload.title: Upload Files
fileUpload.description: Drag and drop GPX, FIT, TCX, or CSV files
fileUpload.supportedFormats: Supported formats: GPX, FIT, TCX, CSV

strava.title: Strava Bulk Export (ZIP)
strava.description: Upload your complete Strava data export ZIP — matches activities.csv with GPX/TCX/FIT files for full trackpoint data
strava.clickToUpload: Click to upload your Strava export ZIP
strava.downloadInstructions: Download from strava.com → Settings → My Account → Download or Delete Your Account
strava.fullTrackpointDataDesc: {count} activities now include per-second GPS coordinates, heart rate, cadence, and power data.
strava.step1: Go to strava.com → Settings → My Account → Download or Delete Your Account
strava.step2: Request your data export (Strava will email you a ZIP when ready)
strava.step3: Upload the ZIP here — activities are matched with their GPX files by activity ID
strava.step4: Activities with GPX files get full trackpoint data: GPS route, HR at every second, cadence, and power
strava.safeToReupload: Safe to re-upload: If you have already imported via CSV, uploading the full ZIP will upgrade those activities with rich trackpoint data — no duplicates created.

csv.title: CSV Bulk Import
csv.description: Upload your activities.csv from a data export

gpx.title: GPX / TCX / FIT File Import
gpx.description: Upload .gpx, .tcx, or .fit files from your devices or other platforms
gpx.clickToUpload: Click to upload GPX, TCX, or FIT files
gpx.supportedDevices: Supports Garmin, Wahoo, Suunto, COROS, and other device exports
gpx.supportedFormats: Supported formats: GPX (GPS Exchange Format), TCX (Garmin Training Center XML), FIT (Garmin Flexible & Interoperable Data Transfer). FIT files are Garmin's native binary format — used by most modern Garmin watches, Edge cycling computers, and many COROS/Wahoo devices.

manual.title: Manual Activity Entry
manual.description: Log a training session that wasn't tracked digitally

duplicates.title: Duplicate Activities
duplicates.description: Review and merge duplicate activities detected across your data sources.

admin.title: Admin Panel
admin.description: Manage users, integrations, and system settings

email.title: Resend Configuration
email.description: Configure your Resend API key for password reset emails and system notifications. Get your API key from the Resend dashboard. Make sure your domain is verified and you've created an API key with sending permissions.
email.fromDescription: Must be a verified sender in your Resend account.
email.expiryDescription: How long password reset links remain valid (1–168 hours).
email.testDescription: Send a test email to verify your Resend configuration is working.

coach.title: AI Coach
coach.placeholder: Ask a question or request plan changes...
coach.sendHint: Enter to send / Shift+Enter for new line
coach.resetPlanTitle: Delete all future weekly plans and archive this conversation
coach.resetPlanDetail: All future training weeks will be deleted and this conversation archived.
coach.clickToAnalyze: Click Analyze to get training insights from your AI coach.

general.title: General
general.passwordTooShort: New password must be at least 8 characters.
general.trainingContextTitle: Where and When I Can Train
general.trainingContextDesc: Describe your training environment so the AI coach can give you better recommendations.
general.trainingContextLabel: Where and When I Can Train...
general.trainingContextPlaceholder: Describe your training environment in as much detail as possible. For example: which facilities you use (roads, trails, track, pool, gym), when you typically train (mornings, evenings, specific days), any constraints or preferences. The AI coach uses this context for personalized recommendations and analysis.

bodyMetrics.title: Body Metrics
bodyMetrics.description: Track your weight, resting heart rate, and other body measurements over time.
bodyMetrics.needMoreEntries: Need 2+ entries in the last 7 days
bodyMetrics.notesPlaceholder: How you felt, sleep quality, etc.
bodyMetrics.noEntriesDesc: Start tracking your weight, resting heart rate, and more to see trends over time.

goals.title: Goals
goals.description: Set your target races and track your progress.
goals.noGoalsDesc: Set your first race goal to get personalized training plans.
goals.name: Name
goals.goalStatementPlaceholder: What do you want to accomplish? e.g. "Finish under 12 hours", "Top 10 in age group"
goals.courseProfileReplaceHint: Upload a new file to replace the course profile.
goals.courseProfileUploadHint: Upload a GPX, TCX, or FIT course file to extract elevation profile data for more accurate training recommendations.

integrations.title: Integrations
integrations.description: Connect external services to auto-import your training and health data.

coros.title: COROS Training Hub
coros.description: Sync your activities from COROS watches directly via the COROS Training Hub.
coros.backgroundSyncInfo: Background sync runs every 4 hours. Use "Sync Now" to trigger an immediate sync.
coros.passwordNote: Your password is used only for initial authentication. An access token is stored securely and your password is not saved.
coros.disconnectConfirm: Disconnect COROS? This will remove all synced activities.
coros.resetSyncConfirm: Reset COROS sync state? The next sync will re-fetch all activities from COROS.
coros.resetSyncComplete: Sync state reset. Use 'Sync Now' to re-fetch all data.

garmin.title: Garmin Connect
garmin.description: Sync activities and daily health metrics (heart rate, sleep, stress, HRV, body battery) from your Garmin devices.
garmin.backgroundSyncInfo: Background sync runs every 4 hours. Use "Sync Now" to trigger an immediate sync.
garmin.mfaHelpText: Check your authenticator app or email for the verification code from Garmin.
garmin.mfaMessage: Multi-factor authentication is enabled. Enter the code from your authenticator app or email.
garmin.mfaLoginHelp: Enter the verification code from your Garmin account to complete the login.
garmin.passwordNote: Your password is used only for initial authentication. OAuth tokens are stored securely and your password is not saved. Garmin sessions typically last about a year before requiring re-authentication.
garmin.disconnectConfirm: Disconnect Garmin? This will remove all synced activities and health data.
garmin.resetSyncConfirm: Reset Garmin sync state? The next sync will re-fetch all activities and health data from the last 30 days.
garmin.resetSyncComplete: Sync state reset. Use 'Sync Now' to re-fetch all recent data.

backupRestore.title: Backup & Restore
backupRestore.description: Export all your data as a .tar.gz archive, or restore from a previous backup to a new account.

backupCard.title: Download Backup
backupCard.description: Export all your data — activities, goals, settings, GPS data, and more — as a compressed archive (.tar.gz).
backupCard.preparingHint: This may take a minute depending on the amount of data. The download link will appear here when it's ready.
backupCard.includedData: The backup includes: activities, GPS track data (one file per activity), race goals, facilities, body metrics, training schedule, weekly assessments, weekly plans, fatigue alerts, daily health data, analysis reports, API keys, AI provider credentials, and Garmin Connect integration tokens.

restoreCard.title: Restore from Backup
restoreCard.description: Upload a backup file (.tar.gz) to restore your data to this account.
restoreCard.warning: This will replace all existing data in your account with the data from the backup.
restoreCard.helpText: Use this to migrate your data to a new account. Sign up, then come here to restore from a backup you downloaded from your previous account.
restoreCard.restoreCompleteWithCount: Restore complete. {total} records imported across {dataTypes} data types.

dangerZone.title: Danger Zone
dangerZone.description: Permanently delete selected data types. These actions cannot be undone.
dangerZone.wipeDescription: Choose the types of data you want to delete, then confirm. Your account will remain active.
dangerZone.confirmDescription: This action is irreversible. The following data will be permanently deleted:
dangerZone.deletedSuccess: Deleted: {summary}. Your account is still active.
dangerZone.wipeError: Failed to wipe data. Please try again.

credentials.title: API & Credentials
credentials.description: Manage your API keys and AI provider configuration.
credentials.aiProviderDescription: Choose your preferred AI provider, configure your key, and test the connection — all in one place. All AI coaching features use your key, or a server-default DeepSeek key when set.
credentials.serverDefaultNotice: Server default: DeepSeek (deepseek-v4-flash) is configured. AI features work out of the box. Set your own key below to override.
credentials.configuredNotice: Your AI provider is configured. Change settings below to update.
credentials.apiKeyPlaceholderReplace: Enter new key to replace saved one
credentials.apiKeyDescription: Your key is stored in the database and never shared with other users.
credentials.ollamaKeyDescription: Ollama runs locally — a placeholder key is fine.
credentials.apiKeysDescription: API keys let external tools and scripts push activity data to your account programmatically.
credentials.pushApiDescription: Upload GPX, TCX, or FIT files to your account from any HTTP client. Send the file in the request body and authenticate with your API key.
credentials.paramExternalIdDescription: Deduplication key (default: hash of filename + date)
credentials.createKeyPlaceholder: e.g. Watch Push, Zapier, iOS Shortcut
credentials.keyCreatedDescription: This is the only time the full key will be shown. Store it securely.
credentials.keyWarning: This key grants full access to push activities to your account. Do not share it.

analysis.title: Analysis
analysis.description: Configure how often the AI coach analyzes your training and generates insights.
analysis.frequencyLabel: How often should the AI analyze your training?
analysis.descActivityCount: Analyze after every N new activities are synced or logged.
analysis.descEveryNDays: Analyze every N days regardless of activity volume.
analysis.descDaily: Generate fresh analysis and plan adjustments every day.
analysis.descWeekly: Analyze each week on your configured review day and time.
analysis.descMonthly: Analyze on a specific day of each month.
analysis.activitiesBetweenDescription: Analysis will run after every {count} new activit{count, plural, one {y} other {ies}}.
analysis.daysBetweenDescription: Analysis will run every {count} day{count, plural, one {} other {s}}.
analysis.dayOfMonthDescription: Analysis will run on the {day, selectordinal, one {#st} two {#nd} few {#rd} other {#th}} of each month.
analysis.reviewTimeDescription: The analysis will run at this time on the scheduled day, within an hour of the trigger condition being met.
analysis.reviewTimeDescriptionSimple: The analysis will run at this time on the scheduled day.
analysis.noAnalysis: No analysis yet — generate your first coach notes from the dashboard.
analysis.howItWorksDescription: The AI analysis reviews your training logs, PMC metrics (CTL/ATL/TSB), race goals, health data (sleep, HRV, stress), and fatigue status to generate personalized coaching insights.
analysis.itemCoachNotes: Coach’s notes — a detailed analysis of your training trajectory
analysis.itemDataDrivers: Data drivers — what metrics influenced the analysis (CTL, TSB, HRV, etc.)
analysis.itemRaceReadiness: Race readiness — how prepared you are for each goal race
analysis.itemRecommendations: Recommendations — actionable advice for the coming week
analysis.llmNote: Note: Analysis requires an active LLM provider configuration in API & Credentials.

signIn.title: Welcome back
signIn.description: Sign in to continue your training journey
signIn.forgotNotEnabled: This feature is not enabled. Please contact your administrator.

signUp.title: Create your account
signUp.errorSignIn: Account created but sign-in failed. Please try signing in.
signUp.errorNetwork: Something went wrong. Please try again.

resetPassword.invalidLinkDesc: No reset token provided. Ask your admin for a new password reset link.
resetPassword.successDesc: Your password has been changed. Redirecting to sign in...
